courses.title: Test ml (ml)
courses.overview: ഈ കോഴ്സ് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ അടിസ്ഥാനങ്ങൾ, ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ്, നാച്ചുറൽ ലാംഗ്വേജ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ് എന്നിവയുൾപ്പെടെയുള്ള വിവിധ വശങ്ങൾ കവർ ചെയ്യുന്നു. കോഴ്സിന്റെ ലക്ഷ്യം പുതുമുഖങ്ങൾക്ക് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ സമഗ്രമായ അവബോധം നൽകുകയും സങ്കീർണ്ണമായ ഡാറ്റ പ്രശ്നങ്ങൾ പരിഹരിക്കുന്നതിന് അവരെ സജ്ജമാക്കുകയും ചെയ്യുക എന്നതാണ്. ഈ കോഴ്സ് പൂർത്തിയാകുമ്പോൾ, നിങ്ങൾക്ക് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് ആൽഗോരിതങ്ങൾ ഡിസൈൻ ചെയ്യാനും നടപ്പിലാക്കാനും കഴിയും.
learning_outcomes: മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ അടിസ്ഥാന സൂത്രവാക്യങ്ങളും ആശയങ്ങളും മനസ്സിലാക്കുക.
ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗിന്റെ അടിസ്ഥാനങ്ങൾ, ന്യൂറൽ നെറ്റ്വർക്കുകൾ, കൺവോള്യൂഷണൽ നെറ്റ്വർക്കുകൾ എന്നിവ പഠിക്കുക.
നാച്ചുറൽ ലാംഗ്വേജ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ്, ക്ലാസിഫിക്കേഷൻ, റെഗ്രേഷൻ എന്നിവയുൾപ്പെടെയുള്ള മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് ആപ്ലിക്കേഷനുകൾ.
മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് മോഡലുകൾ വികസിപ്പിക്കുന്നതിനും മെച്ചപ്പെടുത്തുന്നതിനുമുള്ള സാങ്കേതികവിദ്യകൾ പഠിക്കുക.

chapters.title: മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് അവബോധം
chapters.content: ഈ അദ്ധ്യായം മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ അടിസ്ഥാനകാഴ്ചപ്പാടുകൾ, മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ നിർവചനം, മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ വെവ്വേറിട്ടുള്ള തരങ്ങൾ, മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ ചരിത്രം എന്നിവ ഉൾക്കൊള്ളുന്നു. കൂടാതെ, മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് ആൽഗോരിതങ്ങളുടെ പ്രയോഗങ്ങളും മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് മേഖലയിലെ പ്രധാന സംഭവവികാസങ്ങളും ചർച്ച ചെയ്യുന്നു. മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് എന്താണെന്നും അതിന്റെ പ്രാധാന്യം എന്തെന്നും മനസ്സിലാക്കുന്നതിന് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് ദർശനത്തിന്റെ ഒരു അവലോകനം ഇത് നൽകുന്നു. മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ സംഗ്രഹം നൽകുന്നതിന് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് പ്രക്രിയയുടെ ഘട്ടങ്ങളും ചർച്ചചെയ്യപ്പെടുന്നു.

chapters.title: ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ്
chapters.content: ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ് എന്നത് മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗിന്റെ ഒരു ഉപവിഭാഗമാണ്, ഇത് ന്യൂറൽ നെറ്റ്വർക്കുകളുടെ സങ്കീർണ്ണതകൾക്കും ഡീപ്തയുടെ കമ്പ്യൂട്ടേഷണൽ മോഡലുകൾക്കും ശ്രദ്ധ കേന്ദ്രീകരിക്കുന്നു. ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ് സാങ്കേതികവിദ്യകൾ ഇമേജ് റെക്കഗ്നിഷൻ, സ്പീച്ച് റെക്കഗ്നിഷൻ, നാച്ചുറൽ ലാംഗ്വേജ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ് തുടങ്ങിയ മേഖലകളിൽ വിപ്ലവം സൃഷ്ടിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. ഈ അദ്ധ്യായം ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗിന്റെ അടിസ്ഥാന കാഴ്ചപ്പാടുകൾ, ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ് ആർക്കിടെക്ചറുകൾ, ഡീപ് ലേണിംഗ് ആൽഗോരിതങ്ങളുടെ നടപ്പിലാക്കൽ എന്നിവയെ കേന്ദ്രീകരിക്കുന്നു.

chapters.title: നാച്ചുറൽ ലാംഗ്വേജ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ്
chapters.content: നാച്ചുറൽ ലാംഗ്വേജ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ് (NLP) എന്നത് കമ്പ്യൂട്ടറുകളും മനുഷ്യരും തമ്മിലുള്ള ആശയവിനിമയം സുഗമമാക്കുന്നതിന് ലക്ഷ്യമിടുന്ന ഒരു മെഷീൻ ലേണിംഗ് മേഖലയാണ്. NLP യിൽ ടെക്സ്റ്റ് പ്രോസസ്സിംഗ്, സെന്റിമെന്റ് വിശകലനം, യാഥാർത്ഥ്യ സംഭാഷണ സംവിധാനങ്ങൾ എന്നിവ ഉൾപ്പെടുന്നു. ഈ അദ്ധ്യായം NLP യുടെ അടിസ്ഥാനകാഴ്ചപ്പാടുകൾ, NLP യിലെ പ്രധാന സാങ്കേതികവിദ്യകൾ, NLP യുടെ പ്രയോഗങ്ങൾ എന്നിവയെ കേന്ദ്രീകരിക്കുന്നു.

courses.title: Test (en)
courses.overview: This comprehensive course on testing is designed for beginners, covering the fundamentals of testing, its importance in software development, and the various types of testing. The course aims to provide a thorough understanding of the testing process, enabling learners to apply their knowledge in real-world scenarios. By the end of the course, learners will be equipped with the skills to create effective test cases and execute them efficiently.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basics of software testing and its significance in the software development life cycle
Identify and explain different types of testing, including black box, white box, and gray box testing
Learn to create test cases and test scripts using various testing techniques and tools
Apply testing principles to real-world scenarios and develop effective testing strategies

chapters.title: Introduction to Software Testing
chapters.content: Software testing is an essential part of the software development life cycle. It involves evaluating software applications to ensure they meet the required standards and are free from defects. Testing helps to identify errors, bugs, and inconsistencies in the software, allowing developers to fix them before the product is released to the market. There are several types of testing, including functional testing, performance testing, security testing, and usability testing. Functional testing focuses on the functionality of the software, ensuring that it works as expected. Performance testing, on the other hand, evaluates the software's performance under various conditions, such as heavy traffic or large data sets. Security testing is critical in today's digital age, as it helps to identify vulnerabilities in the software that could be exploited by hackers. Usability testing is also important, as it ensures that the software is user-friendly and easy to navigate. In this chapter, we will delve deeper into the world of software testing, exploring its importance, types, and methodologies. We will also discuss the different testing levels, including unit testing, integration testing, system testing, and acceptance testing. By the end of this chapter, learners will have a solid understanding of the basics of software testing and its significance in the software development life cycle. The importance of testing cannot be overstated, as it helps to ensure that software applications are reliable, stable, and meet the required standards. Testing is an ongoing process that involves continuous evaluation and improvement of the software. It is a critical component of the software development life cycle, and its significance cannot be ignored. In addition to its importance, testing also has several benefits, including improved quality, reduced costs, and increased customer satisfaction. By investing in testing, organizations can ensure that their software applications are of high quality, reliable, and meet the required standards. This, in turn, can lead to increased customer satisfaction, reduced costs, and improved reputation.

chapters.title: Types of Testing
chapters.content: There are several types of testing, each with its own unique characteristics and objectives. Black box testing, also known as functional testing, involves testing the software without knowing its internal workings. This type of testing focuses on the functionality of the software, ensuring that it works as expected. White box testing, on the other hand, involves testing the software with knowledge of its internal workings. This type of testing focuses on the internal structure and workings of the software, ensuring that it is efficient and effective. Gray box testing is a combination of black box and white box testing, where the tester has some knowledge of the internal workings of the software. This type of testing is useful when the tester needs to understand the internal workings of the software, but does not have access to the source code. In addition to these types of testing, there are also other types, such as performance testing, security testing, and usability testing. Performance testing involves evaluating the software's performance under various conditions, such as heavy traffic or large data sets. Security testing involves identifying vulnerabilities in the software that could be exploited by hackers. Usability testing involves evaluating the software's user interface, ensuring that it is user-friendly and easy to navigate. Each type of testing has its own unique objectives and characteristics, and testers must be aware of these differences to ensure that they are testing the software effectively. By understanding the different types of testing, testers can develop effective testing strategies that meet the required standards. The choice of testing type depends on the software application, its requirements, and the testing objectives. Testers must be flexible and adapt to the changing requirements of the software, ensuring that they are testing the software effectively. In this chapter, we will explore the different types of testing in more detail, discussing their characteristics, objectives, and benefits. We will also discuss the challenges and limitations of each type of testing, providing learners with a comprehensive understanding of the different types of testing.

chapters.title: Test Case Development
chapters.content: Test case development is an essential part of the testing process. A test case is a set of inputs, expected results, and execution conditions that are used to test a particular aspect of the software. Test cases are developed based on the software's requirements and specifications, and they are used to evaluate the software's functionality, performance, and security. There are several techniques for developing test cases, including equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, and state transition testing. Equivalence partitioning involves dividing the input data into partitions based on the software's requirements, and then selecting a representative value from each partition. Boundary value analysis involves testing the software at the boundaries of the input data, such as the minimum and maximum values. State transition testing involves testing the software's state transitions, ensuring that it moves from one state to another correctly. In addition to these techniques, there are also other techniques, such as decision table-based testing and cause-effect graphing. Decision table-based testing involves using decision tables to identify the test cases, while cause-effect graphing involves using graphs to identify the cause-and-effect relationships between the inputs and outputs. By using these techniques, testers can develop effective test cases that cover the software's functionality, performance, and security. Test case development is an ongoing process that involves continuous evaluation and improvement of the test cases. Testers must be aware of the software's changing requirements and adapt the test cases accordingly. In this chapter, we will discuss the different techniques for developing test cases, providing learners with a comprehensive understanding of test case development. We will also discuss the challenges and limitations of test case development, providing learners with practical tips and best practices for developing effective test cases.

chapters.title: Test Execution and Automation
chapters.content: Test execution is the process of running the test cases and evaluating the results. It involves executing the test cases, comparing the actual results with the expected results, and reporting any defects or discrepancies. Test execution can be manual or automated, depending on the testing objectives and requirements. Manual testing involves executing the test cases manually, while automated testing involves using tools and scripts to execute the test cases. Automated testing is useful for repetitive and time-consuming tasks, such as regression testing and performance testing. There are several tools and frameworks available for automated testing, including Selenium, Appium, and TestComplete. Selenium is a popular tool for automating web applications, while Appium is a popular tool for automating mobile applications. TestComplete is a comprehensive tool that supports multiple platforms and technologies. In addition to these tools, there are also other tools and frameworks available, such as TestNG and PyUnit. TestNG is a popular framework for automating Java applications, while PyUnit is a popular framework for automating Python applications. By using these tools and frameworks, testers can automate the testing process, reducing the time and effort required for testing. Test execution is an ongoing process that involves continuous evaluation and improvement of the test cases and test scripts. Testers must be aware of the software's changing requirements and adapt the test cases and test scripts accordingly. In this chapter, we will discuss the different approaches to test execution and automation, providing learners with a comprehensive understanding of the testing process. We will also discuss the challenges and limitations of test execution and automation, providing learners with practical tips and best practices for executing and automating tests.

courses.title: Machine Learning (en)
courses.overview: Machine Learning is explained in a practical, beginner-friendly path with examples and chapter-wise progression in en.
learning_outcomes: Understand the core fundamentals of Machine Learning
Break down important concepts into easy mental models
Apply Machine Learning in common real-world scenarios
Build confidence through progressive chapter exercises

chapters.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
chapters.content: Machine Learning starts with understanding why it matters, where it is used, and how beginners can approach it without confusion.

chapters.title: Core Concepts of Machine Learning
chapters.content: This chapter explains the building blocks of Machine Learning with small examples and intuitive step-by-step breakdowns.

chapters.title: Practice Applications of Machine Learning
chapters.content: Use practical scenarios to apply Machine Learning, identify mistakes, and improve with guided practice patterns.

chapters.title: Machine Learning in Real Projects
chapters.content: Learn how Machine Learning appears in real products, how to reason through trade-offs, and how to continue learning independently.
